session_history.content: SSO login loop after certificate rotation — user gets redirected back to login page repeatedly

This is typically caused by a certificate mismatch after rotation. The new certificate needs to be registered in your identity provider's SAML/OIDC configuration. Check that the thumbprint in your SSO settings matches the newly rotated certificate.

query: We tried that but the certificate thumbprint matches. The loop only happens for users in the EU region. Could it be region-specific?

session_history.content: SSO login loop after certificate rotation — user gets redirected back to login page repeatedly
This is typically caused by a certificate mismatch after rotation. Check that the thumbprint in your SSO settings matches the newly rotated certificate.
Can you be more specific about which certificate store I should check?

Check the certificate in your identity provider's SAML configuration. Verify the thumbprint under Azure AD > Enterprise Applications > SAML Signing Certificate. Also check the local certificate store on your ADFS server if using federated auth.

query: OK so the connector is set up. Now during the first sync it pulled 500 work items but only 200 showed up in search. What happened to the rest?

session_history.content: How do I configure webhook notifications for Azure DevOps work item updates?

To set up ADO webhooks, go to Admin > Connectors > New Connector and select Azure DevOps. You'll need a PAT with read access to work items and code. Configure the service hook URL and select the work item update event type.
What permissions does the PAT need exactly?

The PAT needs Work Items (Read), Code (Read), and Wiki (Read) scopes at minimum. For service hooks, you also need the ServiceHooks (Read & Write) scope. Set the PAT to expire within your organization's security policy.

query: The file was a 200-page PDF technical manual. Could the size be the problem?

session_history.content: SharePoint file uploaded 2 hours ago not appearing in search results despite successful sync

There are several reasons a recently synced file might not appear in search. First, check that the delta sync has picked up the change. The sync interval depends on your connector schedule. Also verify the file type is supported for text extraction.
The sync log shows the file was fetched successfully. It's a PDF file.

If the PDF was fetched but isn't appearing in search, the issue may be in text extraction or indexing. Check the ingestion logs for any extraction errors for that document. PDF extraction uses PdfPig and can fail on scanned/image-only PDFs.

query: None of those steps worked. The database CPU is still at 98% and now we're getting connection pool exhaustion errors too. This is affecting all tenants.

session_history.content: Production database CPU at 98% and queries timing out after latest deployment

This is a critical issue. Immediate steps: 1) Check for long-running queries using sys.dm_exec_requests. 2) Review the latest deployment for any schema changes or new query patterns. 3) Consider scaling up the database tier temporarily.
We found some long-running queries but killing them only helps temporarily. They keep coming back.

The recurring queries suggest a code-level issue. Check if the latest deployment introduced any missing indexes or N+1 query patterns. Review the Entity Framework query logs for the affected endpoints. You may also want to add query timeouts as a temporary safeguard.

query: We've tried all the suggested steps over the past 3 days but the webhook delivery keeps failing. At this point our ADO data is days out of date. We need someone to look at the infrastructure.

session_history.content: SSL certificate for our webhook endpoint expired and now we're missing ADO notifications. What do we do?

You need to renew the SSL certificate for your webhook endpoint. Once renewed, update the certificate in your reverse proxy or load balancer. ADO should automatically retry failed deliveries. In the meantime, you can trigger a manual sync to catch up on missed notifications.
We renewed the cert but webhook deliveries are still failing with connection refused errors.

After certificate renewal, verify the endpoint is accessible externally. Check firewall rules, ensure the port is open, and test with curl from an external network. Also verify the webhook URL in ADO service hooks is correct and uses HTTPS with the new certificate.
The endpoint responds to curl fine. ADO test delivery works once manually but automated deliveries keep failing.

This could be a rate limiting or timeout issue on ADO's side. Check if your endpoint responds within the 5-second timeout ADO requires. Also verify there's no WAF or DDoS protection blocking repeated automated requests from Azure DevOps IP ranges.

query: Actually, switching topics — how do I export our audit logs? We have a compliance review coming up next week.

session_history.content: How do I set up per-tenant PII redaction policies with custom regex patterns?

To configure PII redaction policies, go to Admin > Privacy > PII Policies. You can set the mode to Redact, Detect, or Disabled per tenant. For custom patterns, add regex rules under the Custom Patterns section. Each pattern needs a name, regex, and replacement placeholder.
Great, I set up a custom pattern for our internal employee IDs. How do I test it?

You can test PII patterns by using the privacy API's detection endpoint. Send a sample text containing your employee ID format and verify the detection response identifies it correctly. You can also check the ingestion logs for any documents processed after the pattern was added.

query: Wait, before the audit export — going back to PII redaction, will the custom patterns I just configured apply retroactively to already-indexed documents?

session_history.content: How do I set up per-tenant PII redaction policies with custom regex patterns?

To configure PII redaction policies, go to Admin > Privacy > PII Policies. You can set the mode to Redact, Detect, or Disabled per tenant. For custom patterns, add regex rules under the Custom Patterns section.
Great, I set up a custom pattern for our internal employee IDs. How do I test it?
You can test PII patterns by using the privacy API's detection endpoint. Send a sample text and verify detection.
Actually, switching topics — how do I export our audit logs? We have a compliance review coming up next week.
To export audit logs, use the Admin > Compliance > Audit Export feature or call the audit API endpoint. Exports are in NDJSON format and can be filtered by date range, event type, and actor.

query: The pattern says to restart the API service, but earlier you mentioned the other pattern says never restart during business hours. Which one should I follow? It's 2 PM right now.

session_history.content: Two case patterns appear to contradict each other — one says restart the service, the other says never restart. How are conflicts resolved?

Pattern contradictions are detected by the pattern maintenance system using token overlap analysis on symptoms, problems, and resolutions. When contradictions are found, both patterns are flagged for human review. The support lead can then update, deprecate, or merge the conflicting patterns. Trust state transitions require review approval.
I see both patterns are in Approved state. Which one takes precedence for a specific customer case right now?

When multiple approved patterns apply, the system presents both with their confidence scores. The agent should evaluate which pattern better matches the specific symptoms. Consider recency, the number of successful applications, and any environmental conditions mentioned in each pattern.

query: Now that the connector is working, I want to set up a completely different thing — what are the confidence level thresholds and badge colors?

session_history.content: How do I connect a HubSpot account as a knowledge source for customer ticket data?

To connect HubSpot, go to Admin > Connectors > New Connector and select HubSpot. You'll need an API key or OAuth credentials with Tickets read scope. Configure the ticket pipeline you want to sync and set the sync schedule.
The connector shows as Connected but no tickets are appearing. The sync log is empty.

If the sync log is empty, the initial sync may not have triggered yet. Check the connector's sync schedule and try triggering a manual sync. Also verify the HubSpot API key has the correct scopes and that the selected pipeline contains tickets.

query: What about the embedding cache — is that also affected by the index migration, or is it independent?

session_history.content: How does the search schema versioning work? Can we roll back if something goes wrong?

Search schema versioning uses blue-green index migration. When the schema changes, a new index is created alongside the existing one. Documents are reindexed in batches to the new index. Once complete, the alias switches to the new index. The old index is retained for rollback.
During the migration, do search queries still work or is there downtime?

Search continues working during migration. Queries hit the current active index while the new index is being populated. The switchover is atomic via alias update, so there's no downtime. If the migration fails, the old index remains active.
Got it. And the synonym maps — do they carry over to the new index automatically?

Yes, synonym maps are re-applied to the new index during migration. The migration service reads the current synonym map configuration and applies it to the new index schema. Per-tenant synonym rules are preserved.

query: I've now explained the full situation across multiple messages. Given everything — the 403 errors, the missing wiki pages, and the PAT scope issue — can you summarize what we need to fix and in what order?

session_history.content: Connector sync failed with HTTP 403 Forbidden when accessing ADO wiki pages

A 403 error when accessing ADO wiki pages typically indicates the PAT lacks the Wiki (Read) scope. Verify your PAT has this permission. Also check that the service account has access to the specific project's wiki.
The PAT has Wiki Read scope. But we also noticed some wiki pages in a different project are returning 403 too.

If multiple projects return 403, the PAT may be scoped to specific projects rather than the full organization. Check the PAT's organization access setting — it should be set to 'All accessible organizations' or include all target projects.
You're right, the PAT was project-scoped. We also noticed that code wiki pages work fine but provisioned wikis fail. Is there a different permission needed?

Yes, provisioned wikis (published from a Git repository) require Code (Read) scope in addition to Wiki (Read). This is because provisioned wikis are backed by Git repos. Ensure the PAT has both scopes.
We also have 50 wiki pages that were synced before but disappeared from search after we updated the connector settings last week.

When connector settings are updated, a re-sync may be needed. The previously indexed pages may have been invalidated. Trigger a full sync (not delta) to reindex all wiki content. Check if the updated settings changed the scope or project filter, which could exclude previously included pages.
